2606.11361.title: A PubMed-Scale Dataset of Structured Biomedical Abstracts
2606.11361.paper_summary: Structured abstracts are important for biomedical literature processing, by facilitating information retrieval, text mining, and knowledge synthesis. However, a vast portion of abstracts indexed in PubMed remain unstructured, presenting a significant bottleneck for downstream text-processing workflows and applications. To resolve this limitation, we introduce Structured PubMed, a comprehensive corpus of section-labeled biomedical abstracts compiled from the complete PubMed database, encompassing over 23.2 million research-article records. The corpus is divided into two distinct subsets: a collection of 5.9 million author-structured abstracts parsed from official XML files, and an automatically labeled collection of 17.2 million originally unstructured abstracts structured via a verbatim-extraction Large Language Model pipeline. Every record is harmonized under a unified five-section schema and mapped to its original PubMed identifier, publication type, and publication date. This dataset can be utilized to train sentence-classification models, benchmark text-segmentation architectures, and perform large-scale, section-specific information extraction at an unprecedented PubMed-wide scale.
2606.11361.paper_summary_zh: 結構化摘要對於生物醫學文獻處理非常重要，因為它促進了信息檢索、文本挖掘和知識綜合。<lb/>然而，PubMed中索引的絕大部分摘要仍然是非結構化的，這對下游文本處理工作流程和應用程序造成了重大瓶頸。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們推出了結構化PubMed，這是一個全面的生物醫學摘要語料庫，包含來自完整PubMed數據庫的部分標記摘要，涵蓋超過2320萬條研究文章記錄。<lb/>該語料庫分為兩個不同的子集：一個是從官方XML文件中解析出的590萬條作者結構化摘要的集合，另一個是通過逐字提取大型語言模型管道結構化的1720萬條最初非結構化摘要的自動標記集合。<lb/>每條記錄都在統一的五部分架構下進行了協調，並映射到其原始的PubMed標識符、出版類型和出版日期。<lb/>這個數據集可以用來訓練句子分類模型、基準文本分割架構，並在前所未有的PubMed範圍內進行大規模、特定部分的信息提取。
2606.11361.author: Chia-Hsuan Chang et.al.
2606.11361.authors: Chia-Hsuan Chang, Haerin Song, Brian Ondov, Hua Xu
